user_query:
I’m preparing to publish my POLI 402 course site and a small dataset arguing that political structures predominate over religious ones in shaping social systems. I need a pre-publication legal/compliance review of the local materials in input/. Please produce three outputs, all under output/:

1) output/compliance_report.md — A concise, structured report that I can share with our department’s compliance liaison. Include the following sections:
- Overview: one-paragraph summary of what you reviewed and why.
- Findings by Category: organize findings under (a) Student/data privacy; (b) Political activity compliance; (c) Copyright/third-party content; (d) Tracking/analytics; (e) Configuration/site structure issues. For each finding, cite the exact file path and the exact offending substring (or line) that demonstrates the issue, and explain why it violates or risks violating the included campus policy (see input/policies/campus_political_activity_policy.md) or common copyright/privacy constraints. If a required disclaimer is missing, explicitly note which page lacks it.
- Recommended Remediations: for each finding, provide a concrete, testable action (e.g., remove specific fields, redact emails/names, add a specific disclaimer, remove or replace quotes, disable analytics), and where applicable, include the exact disclaimer text required by the included policy.
- Status Summary: a short status update suitable for email/Slack summarizing readiness to publish (e.g., “Blocker/non-blocker” items) and recommended next steps.

2) output/dir_inventory.json — A machine-readable inventory of everything under input/ with basic classification and flags. Represent it as a JSON array where each item has: {"path": "<relative path>", "file_type": "markdown|config|code|data|text|policy|email", "detected_indicators": ["<short flags>"], "notes": "<1–2 line rationale>"}. Detected indicators should be short tokens like EMAIL_ADDRESS, NAME_FIELD, ANALYTICS_ID, POLITICAL_SOLICITATION, MISSING_DISCLAIMER, THIRD_PARTY_QUOTE, or similar, as appropriate to the content you find. Every file in input/ must appear exactly once with at least an empty detected_indicators list. Use the file contents to justify your flags and ensure consistency with the compliance_report.md.

3) output/deployment_request_email.txt — Rewrite the rough request in input/email/deployment_request_draft.txt for tone, clarity, and brevity. The rewritten message must be professional and neutral in political tone, request a staging review rather than immediate publication, summarize key compliance fixes from your report, and propose whether to pause deployment until specified blockers are addressed. Keep it concise (<= 180 words) and suitable for sending to the department IT/web team.

Scope and constraints:
- Use only the provided files; do not use the Internet. Cross-check all findings against the included campus policy excerpt in input/policies/campus_political_activity_policy.md.
- Specifically inspect: (a) input/scripts/collect_responses.py and input/site/mkdocs.yml for code/config issues (e.g., PII collection or analytics IDs), (b) all files in input/site/pages/ for missing disclaimers and political solicitation language, (c) input/data/survey_responses.csv for any PII, and (d) input/site/assets/quotes.txt for third-party content.
- Your report must call out at least the following if present: any names/emails in data or code; any political solicitation language; any missing policy-required disclaimer; any analytics/tracking ID; any third-party quotes without clear permission.
- Do not modify files under input/. Produce only the three outputs above.
 All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
